sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal, probably i.q. סָפָה, יָסַף (compare קָשָׁה, קָשַׁח, and the observations under the word קָלַח), to add.

Niphal, to be added, to join oneself; followed by עַל Isa 14:1.

Pual, to be gathered together, to assemble selves, Job 30:7.

Hithpael, i.q. Niphal; followed by בְּ 1Sa 26:19.

II.

ספח

i.q שָׁפַח, Arab. سفح-

(1) to pour, to pour out (see Piel, and the noun סָפִיחַ ).

(2) to anoint (comp. נָסַךְ; Syr. ܣܪܚ to pour; Aph. to anoint any one a bishop). 1Sa 2:36, סְפָחֵנִי נָא אֶל־אַחַת הַכְּהֻנֹּות “anoint, (i.e. constitute) me, I pray, to some priestly office.”

(3) to spread out, i.q. Eth. ሰፊሐ፡; whence מִסְפָּחָה a cushion, quilt.

Piel, to pour out for some one to drink, Hab 2:15. [This root is not divided into two parts in Thes.].

Derivatives, סָפִיחַ, מִסְפַּחַת, and-
